response:
The error occurs because your WPF project targets the legacy .NET Framework runtime while your main application runs on .NET 7, causing a mismatch in assembly resolution.

You must reconfigure your WPF project file to explicitly target net7.0 or net8.0 instead of the older framework version to ensure compatibility.

cousin projects that rely on WPF in a cross-platform architecture should be refactored to use .NET 7 compatible UI libraries or conditional compilation.

Updating the project file to use the modern SDK style format will allow the compiler to include the correct PresentationFramework assemblies for your target runtime.

After making these changes and restoring your NuGet packages, the application will load the WPF window correctly on Windows without triggering the file not found exception.
